skill.name: image-edit
skill.description: Image-to-image editing through Image Skill's hosted runtime, with guide commands, model selection, hosted assets, job recovery, cost receipts, and feedback.
skill.summary: Run hosted image edits and variations from agent workflows.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, hardcoded URLs, hidden file paths, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found no prompt injection, no malware, and no cryptographic code, but the skill does require remote npm execution and a hosted image API that can receive user media.

medium_findings.title: Remote npm Command Execution Required
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run npx commands for image-skill and skills. This is expected for the workflow, but unpinned remote packages can execute code and should be allowed only in trusted environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command examples are explicit and actionable. The commands appear legitimate for this skill, but remote package execution remains a real supply-chain and local execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Hosted Image Service Receives User Media
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill points agents to Image Skill hosted URLs and a hosted API for image editing. This matches the stated purpose, but user images and generated assets may leave the local environment and persist as hosted media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file repeatedly describes a hosted runtime, hosted media URLs, and a hosted API. This confirms network transfer risk without showing malicious exfiltration intent.

low_findings.title: Workspace Agent Home Reconfiguration
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill suggests setting HOME and CODEX_HOME to hidden workspace directories and creating them. This is a bounded workaround for writable agent state, but it can change where future agent configuration is read or written.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem commands are directly present and affect hidden directories. The scope appears limited to the current workspace, reducing severity.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at the YAML description and token description. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or encryption implementation appears at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic code in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Service References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs identify the homepage, docs, canonical skill, and hosted API for the intended service. They are not suspicious by themselves, but the hosted API risk is covered separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are consistent with the declared Image Skill service and documentation. No evidence found of covert endpoints or mismatched domains.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned npx Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The commands use npx with package names and latest resolution. This can run package code from the npm ecosystem during normal skill use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in multiple command examples. The risk is supply-chain execution, not confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hosted API Media Processing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on hosted Image Skill endpoints and durable hosted media. Sensitive or private images should not be processed without user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hosted API and hosted media behavior are described directly. This confirms data-transfer risk while leaving service trust to policy review.

content.user_title: Edit Images Through Hosted AI Models
content.value_statement: Agents often need image edits without managing provider accounts or local model servers. This skill routes edit requests through Image Skill's hosted runtime with job tracking and receipts.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents through no-spend readiness checks before paid image edits.
Runs hosted image-to-image edit commands through the image-skill CLI.
Supports model selection for OpenAI GPT image edit workflows.
Returns hosted asset URLs, job identifiers, cost receipts, and JSON results.
Provides feedback instructions when a needed capability is missing.
limitations: Requires network access to Image Skill hosted services.
Uses remote npm packages and a hosted API, so package and service trust matter.
Paid generations require explicit spend approval and available Image Skill credits.
Does not run local image models or support offline editing.

use_cases.title: Revise Campaign Images
use_cases.description: Edit backgrounds, details, or style while preserving the subject for marketing assets.

use_cases.title: Create Product Variations
use_cases.description: Generate controlled image variations for product concepts, listings, or visual tests.

use_cases.title: Automate Agent Image Tasks
use_cases.description: Let coding agents request image edits, receive hosted results, and record cost receipts.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-edit to replace the background of this image with a clean studio setting. Keep the subject unchanged and show the cost before spending.

prompt_templates.title: Style Variation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-edit to create two variations of this image in a polished editorial style. Preserve layout, subject identity, and key colors.

prompt_templates.title: Repair Existing Asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-edit to remove small distractions from this image. Do not change the product, logo, proportions, or visible text.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Hosted Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the image-edit guide output, confirm required inputs, estimate spend, then run the recommended edit command only if media spend is approved.

output_examples.input: Replace a cluttered office background while keeping the person unchanged.
output_examples.output: A hosted edited image with the same subject, a cleaner studio background, job status, and a cost receipt summary.

output_examples.input: Create a product image variation with a brighter surface and softer shadows.
output_examples.output: A hosted variation that keeps the product shape and branding while adjusting surface lighting and presentation.

output_examples.input: Remove distracting objects from a promotional image without changing the main item.
output_examples.output: A cleaned image result, a durable asset link, and notes about any unchanged limitations.

best_practices: Start with the guide command before running any paid image edit.
Get user approval before uploading private images or spending credits.
Write edit prompts with clear preservation rules for subjects, logos, and text.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential images without explicit user consent.
Do not run generated spend commands unless the guide marks them runnable.
Do not use this skill when offline local-only image processing is required.

faq.question: Does this skill require provider API keys?
faq.answer: No. It uses the Image Skill hosted runtime instead of user-supplied provider credentials.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing image?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow focuses on image-to-image edits, variations, and image-conditioned transforms.

faq.question: Does it spend money automatically?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to start with a no-spend guide and run paid commands only when media spend is allowed.

faq.question: Where are generated images stored?
faq.answer: The skill describes durable hosted media URLs through Image Skill, so outputs may be stored by the hosted service.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It depends on remote npm packages and Image Skill hosted services.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review package trust, hosted service policy, image privacy, and credit-spend approval.
